title: DispatchPolicy
description: Deterministic mapping from unit characteristics to an execution plan. Read by the compass to assemble each dispatch — selection is policy, not a model decision. Kernel defines the strategy vocabulary; a kit supplies the rules for its family. First matching rule wins; `default` applies when none match.

family.description: Kit/family this policy binds (e.g., corpus, app).

rules.description: Ordered match rules. First whose `match` is satisfied supplies the plan.

match.description: Unit characteristics to match. Omitted keys are wildcards.

posture.note: Default is maximal decomposition along real seams: prefer orchestrator-workers/team/parallelization and sub-agent delegation wherever the work admits them. A rule (or the default) collapses to a simpler shape only when justified by ABSENCE of real structure — an irreducible unit, no independent seams, or depth past the fidelity limit — never by timidity. Width is maximized; depth is bounded by delegation.max_depth; budget always binds.

ExecutionPlan.description: The typed plan a DispatchAdapter executes. Extends the dispatch contract beyond budget + skill surface.

orchestration_shape.description: Topology of model calls inside the unit. Start simplest; escalate only on measured failure.

loop_strategy.description: Iteration discipline. ralph-fresh-context may wrap any of the others for long-horizon units.

retrieval.description: Just-in-time substrate to load. 'broad' is discouraged (context hoarding).

compaction.description: Compaction is a last resort; prefer fresh-context handoff.

working_set_only.description: Durable state stays on disk; only the active working set occupies context.

model_tier.description: Generator model tier. Critic tier is set separately by the verification policy.

max_iterations.description: Hard iteration cap; escalate quality-per-iteration before raising this.

mode.description: sub-agents = dispatched children (one cell each); team = role-specialized peers with typed handoffs.

max_depth.description: Hard cap on delegation depth — handoff fidelity is multiplicative; deep nesting loses more than it gains.
